nativeRange.habitat: Generally found in tropical and subtropical ponds, slow streams, ditches, and shallow lake margins across the southern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. Grows both fully submersed and emersed along muddy shorelines.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) or root tabs in inert gravel
open midground-to-background planting space for stem clusters
bright lighting (medium to high PAR) for deepest red coloration
iron-rich water-column dosing to push red pigments

summary: A classic North American stem plant and the most widely traded Ludwigia, prized for greenish-bronze to red coloration that intensifies under brighter light and iron-rich dosing.
careNotes: Ludwigia repens is the type form behind most 'Red Ludwigia' trade entries and is generally considered one of the easiest red-capable stem plants for newer aquascapers. Lighting and iron are the main levers for coloration: under low to medium light the plant tends toward green to bronze; under medium-to-high light levels with iron supplementation, the upper leaves and stems develop the deep red color that gives this species its 'Red Ludwigia' trade name. Red pigmentation is broadly reported to appear above roughly 40 PAR, with 100–200 PAR cited as the range that brings out the most saturated color. Placement is midground to background. Nutrient-rich substrate such as aquasoil gives the best results; in inert gravel, root tabs can substitute, and sand is best avoided as it can compact around the roots and restrict uptake. A closely related cultivar, Ludwigia palustris 'Super Red', is more compact and tends to show reliable red color under more modest light conditions, while L. repens is the larger, more vigorous species and is more widely stocked across the trade.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: cut the top of a mature stem (typically just above a node), remove the lowest leaves to expose the node where new roots will emerge, and replant the cutting directly into the substrate. Pinching off the apical bud encourages lateral side shoots, which can be trimmed and replanted in turn for rapid mass propagation. Floating cuttings will root in the water column before planting. Trim regularly to keep stems healthy and to prevent the lower portion from shading out and dropping leaves.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica only carries Ludwigia repens 'Rubin' (item 033D), not the green type form. Used here for cultivar identity (North American origin), height (20-30+ cm), growth rate (medium), light (medium), CO2 (low), and care difficulty (easy). The supplied search URL (tropica.com/en/search?q=Ludwigia+repens) returned only the navigation chrome with no per-species detail; resolved to the cultivar's product detail page.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Confirms scientific name, temperature 71-83 °F (~21.7-28.3 °C), moderate lighting, CO2 not required but beneficial, fast growth, background placement, nutrient-rich substrate helpful, trim-and-replant propagation, easy care level.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, height 4-8 inches (10-20 cm), fast growth, medium light, CO2 not necessary, pH 6.0-7.0, midground-to-background, cuttings propagation, quality aquarium soil recommended, moderate care; notes plant is fragile and sensitive to drastic parameter changes.

additional.name: AquariumSource
additional.notes: Confirms native range (North and Central America, tropical ponds/streams/lakes), height ~20 inches (~50 cm), temperature 68-82 °F, pH 6.0-8.0, hardness 3-8 KH, high-light preference, fast growth, midground-to-background, nutrient-rich substrate (avoid sand), cuttings and emergent-seed propagation, minimum tank 20 gallons (10 gal baseline), beginner-friendly.

additional.name: AquariumStoreDepot
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, US/Mexico/Central America origin, height 8-20 inches (20-51 cm), temperature 72-82 °F (22-28 °C), pH 6.5-7.5, moderate-to-strong lighting (red coloration above ~40 PAR, optimal 100-200 PAR), CO2 beneficial but not required, fast growth, mid/background placement, aqua soil substrate, stem-cutting propagation with lower leaves removed at node, weekly iron supplementation for red color, easy care, ~10 gal minimum tank.

additional.name: AquariumBreeder
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae (willowherb / evening primrose family), southern US and Mexico origin, height up to 50 cm (~20 inches), temperature 20-28 °C (68-82 °F), pH 6.0-8.0, hardness 3-8 GH preferred, low-to-medium light (red varieties need more), CO2 not needed to low, fast growth, mid/background placement, nutrient-rich substrate (sand can suffocate roots), cuttings propagation, easy care, 10 gal (~40 L) minimum tank.
